title: buildah version: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax
body:
**Description**

`buildah version` breaks scripts by exiting with `$? = 1` 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build from sources (22 April 2018)
2. `buildah version` 

**Describe the results you received:**
````bash
strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax
````

**Describe the results you expected:**
````bash
buildah version 0.16 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
````

**Output of `apt list buildah`:**
```
buildah/xenial 0.16-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa5 amd64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**
```
strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=16.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=xenial
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 16.04.4 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="16.04.4 LTS (Xenial Xerus)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 16.04.4 LTS"
VERSION_ID="16.04"
HOME_URL="http://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="http://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
VERSION_CODENAME=xenial
UBUNTU_CODENAME=xenial

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux hedge-p2-1320a 4.4.0-119-generic #143-Ubuntu SMP Mon Apr 2 16:08:24 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
This is really odd.  Version is failing as it can't parse the system date at build time.  I don't have a Ubuntu vm handy, could you try:

date=$(date +%s)
echo $date

and let me know if that spits out a value other than nil or zero @taqtiqa-mark?  Thanks!

@taqtiqa-mark Doesn't help a bunch, but it's at least a datapoint.  Thanks for checking.  I'll spin up an Ubuntu vm Monday and pry further.  I think worse comes to worse I'll change the code to print out some kind of "Build Time Not Resolvable" message rather than causing and error.  All thought that seems a bit gross too.  TY!

@pixdrift Same system.
@TomSweeneyRedHat: Maybe "Buildah version not resolvable"?

In case it helps I can add the `buildah --version` works, so maybe `buildah version` can just be a proxy for `buildah --version`, and just have one code path for this information?

Suggest issue template be adjusted from `apt list buildah` to: `apt list --installed buildah`.  In this case the result is empty:
````bash
$ apt list --installed buildah
> Listing... Done
````

This looks more like a compile time issue than anything else. I suspect the reason @TomSweeneyRedHat asked about `date +%s` is that it is used here in the Makefile, which is then passed into the build to make the final binary:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/Makefile#L10

The error you are seeing is coming from here when it reads the value:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/cmd/buildah/version.go#L24-L28

It's strange that you can get in a situation where that string returns empty, unless something is happening in the build process.

@TomSweeneyRedHat, perhaps it would be better to fail the build process if this value doesn't return as expected?

OK if you think its a build error, when I get time I'll rebuild and log the output.  

Any build flags you want set?  Maybe give the whole build instruction you'd like run.
That's really awkward. How the hell was `version` set to an empty string? :confused:

@ripcurld, that's the $64 question. 

@tatiqa-mark I don't know if there are any build flags that will be especially helpful, but I'd be interested in seeing the output.

Just to thicken the plot, I finally got a chance to spin up an Ubuntu vm this morning and installed by cutting/pasting the install instructions on the https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/install.md and the end results were:

```
root@ubuntutas:~/buildah/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah# buildah version
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.8.7
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    b16a1ea
Built:         Tue Apr 24 07:01:44 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
root@ubuntutas:~/buildah/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah# buildah --version
buildah version 0.16 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
root@ubuntutas:~/buildah/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah# buildah -v
buildah version 0.16 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

I'm going to see what I can do with the Makefile and/or handle the error in version.go a bit better.

I really think it needs a reproducer before changing anything in the version code. I have tried a few different builds and can't get a broken result. The only thing left is removing access to the `date` binary at compile time to see if that reproduces the issue.

Reproducer here:
```
# mv /bin/date /bin/nodate
# date
-bash: date: command not found
# make all
make: date: Command not found
go build -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=aca2a1e -X main.buildInfo=' -o buildah -tags "   selinux seccomp" ./cmd/buildah
go build -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=aca2a1e -X main.buildInfo=' -o imgtype -tags "   selinux seccomp" ./tests/imgtype/imgtype.go
make -C docs
-- truncated --

# ./buildah version
strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

I still believe it's due to `date` not being available at compile time. One explanation may be that it's built in a container without `date`, or for whatever reason permissions on `date` present the build user from accessing it (agree this is strange). I think the `make` should fail if it can't find date.

Thoughts @TomSweeneyRedHat @ripcurld?

@TomSweeneyRedHat, just found another potential issue with a similar fix. If the build server doesn't have git installed the following will fail, returning an empty string:
`GIT_COMMIT := $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)`

The `make` displays an error that git isn't available:
```
$ make all
make: git: Command not found
```

And the result is the following in `buildah version`
```
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    
Built:         Tue Apr 24 22:45:35 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

This doesn't generate an error or non-zero exit code, but it could be included in the Makefile checks too?

I've just posted #616.  I decided to print a warning error and set the date to the epoch.    I think this should be a rare situation and I can't see where having the version set to 12/31/1969 could be problematic for Buildah.

However if other folks think we should instead stop the compilation, I could adjust the PR.

@TomSweeneyRedHat, my only concern would be that this would inadvertently mask the error for someone building it and they wouldn't necessarily be aware that they are producing binaries that aren't 'correct'.

I don't think it's too crazy to have `date` as a build requirement.. interested in what others think.
This should not be able to happen, since the Makefile will blow up without the date or git command.

Thanks everyone. All seems good now:

````
$ buildah version
Version:       1.0
Go Version:    go1.8.7
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    fe204e4
Built:         Thu May 10 04:13:34 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
````

I now see this error message again with:

`buildah version 1.4-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)`
